receivingCourses.title: Introduction to Inquiry and Research in Education

courses.title: Introduction to Inquiry and Research in Education

notes.content: Education is more important than ever and UCLA’s School of Education and Information Studies is at the forefront of that conversation. As the top-ranked public program in education in the nation, the Department of Education is guided by a commitment to integrate theory and practice and to improve educational practice and policy. The department attracts prominent scholars and is internationally recognized for its research centers in evaluation, higher education, child development, and urban education. Whether students choose to pursue a PHD, an EDD, Masters or Bachelors degree, or a teaching or administrative credential, they graduate with a broad understanding of educational theory and tested practice. The Department is known as a leader in the study and practice of urban education, student testing and assessment, teacher and continuing education and development (particularly in urban, multi-ethnic environments), early childhood development, and issues of access, equity, and quality facing K-12 and higher education. Here, faculty and students work together to solve the most pressing issues facing education today. This collaborative, dynamic environment helps students develop the skills they need to become leading education professionals in the 21st century. Transfer applicants interested in the Education & Social Transformation major are admitted to the “pre-major" status and must complete Education 10 or 11 and 35 during their first quarter at UCLA. Transfer students will also need to apply to the Education major during their first quarter at UCLA and before they have completed 135 quarter units. The minimum gpa for UCLA transfer applicants is a 3.2 or higher. Students are encouraged to complete IGETC . For more information regarding this major visi t https://seis.ucla.edu/departments-and-degrees/department-of-education/education-and-social-transformation-major and https://admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
Education is more important than ever and UCLA’s School of Education and Information Studies is at the forefront of that conversation. As the top-ranked public program in education in the nation, the Department of Education is guided by a commitment to integrate theory and practice and to improve educational practice and policy. The department attracts prominent scholars and is internationally recognized for its research centers in evaluation, higher education, child development, and urban education. Whether students choose to pursue a PHD, an EDD, Masters or Bachelors degree, or a teaching or administrative credential, they graduate with a broad understanding of educational theory and tested practice.


The Department is known as a leader in the study and practice of urban education, student testing and assessment, teacher and continuing education and development (particularly in urban, multi-ethnic environments), early childhood development, and issues of access, equity, and quality facing K-12 and higher education. Here, faculty and students work together to solve the most pressing issues facing education today. This collaborative, dynamic environment helps students develop the skills they need to become leading education professionals in the 21st century.


Transfer applicants interested in the Education & Social Transformation major are admitted to the “pre-major" status and must complete Education 10 or 11 and 35 during their first quarter at UCLA. Transfer students will also need to apply to the Education major during their first quarter at UCLA and before they have completed 135 quarter units. The minimum gpa for UCLA transfer applicants is a 3.2 or higher. Students are encouraged to complete IGETC.  


For more information regarding this major visit https://seis.ucla.edu/departments-and-degrees/department-of-education/education-and-social-transformation-major and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: No AP or IB exams are considered equivalent, and credit earned from those exams cannot be used towards the Education and Social Transformation major prep.
